_meta.note: subjective per-session feedback; link to workout by date (session_id optional); energy 1-5 (1=awful,3=ok,5=great); rir_feel=reps_left_in_tank subjective; soreness=body_parts_affected
_meta.fields: d=date; sid=session_id; energy=1-5; rir_feel=text; soreness=[]; wins=[]; flags=[]; coach_note=text; free=freetext

entries.free: rdl: felt stretch in hamstrings — form confirmed good
lower back tight after consecutive days; not injury, likely erector fatigue
first session back after ~3 week break; scale shows 171.0 lb (+3.2 lb); muscle mass highest logged 142.6

entries.rir_feel: high — very gassed; OHP had nothing left
wins: incline DB held 40x10x3 clean despite fatigue — strong signal
triceps and bicep curl unaffected by BJJ fatigue
first BJJ + gym combo week — showed up
flags: bjj_fatigue_carryover: BJJ previous evening caused significant OHP drop (35→30→25); not a regression
ohp_drop_pattern: consistent — do not start OHP at 40 on push day; start at 35
lateral_raise_skipped: machine unavailable again — include explicitly next session
session_cut_short: Neha tired; bicep curl and triceps only partial session
entries.coach_note: OHP collapse (35→30→25) is entirely explained by BJJ shoulder/grip fatigue the night before + 3-week gym gap. Incline DB holding 40x10x3 under these conditions is actually impressive. OHP working weight is 35 on push days — do not attempt 40 on push until 35x10x3 is rock solid across 2 consecutive sessions with no BJJ the night before. Bicep curl 77x10x3 is confirmed — move to 88 next push session.
entries.free: first gym session after starting BJJ (first BJJ class was Jun 22). Felt completely gassed and slow — expected. The carryover from BJJ is real. Plan going forward: push day on Tuesday is the safest gym day since Monday BJJ doesn't heavily load chest/triceps.

wins: incline DB held 40x10x3 clean despite fatigue
triceps pushdown 99x11x3 — above target, progression signal
flags: near_syncope_event: nearly passed out during/after BJJ on 2026-06-29 — combination of heat and severe underfueling
severe_underfueling: only a breakfast burrito + 1 slice of toast consumed for the entire day before BJJ; curry + rice + chicken eaten after BJJ
ohp_drop_pattern_repeat: attempted 40 on push day after BJJ again, collapsed to 30x10x3 sets 2-3 — same pattern as 06-23
bicep_curl_not_tested: hammer curl substituted this session — 77->88 bicep curl progression still unconfirmed
entries.coach_note: Priority item: the near-syncope episode. Eating only a burrito and a slice of toast across a full day before a hard BJJ session, with heat involved, is enough on its own to cause that — independent of fitness level. This needs a fueling fix before the next BJJ session: eat something real in the afternoon before Mon/Wed/Fri classes, even a small carb+protein snack. On the lift side: OHP continuing to collapse at 40 on push day reinforces the existing rule — start at 35 on push day until 35x10x3 is rock solid for 2 consecutive sessions with no BJJ the night before. Triceps pushdown 99x11x3 is ready to progress.
entries.free: treadmill warmup 0.31km/4:06 only; gassed and slow throughout; hammer curl substituted for bicep curl this session
